title: Money moving through your account like a river
subtitle: A short visualisation for building the felt sense of continuous, large cashflow.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation from Day 39 of the MONEY program. The scene: a river of money moving steadily through your account, too wide to dam, too constant to doubt.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.

practice.whenToUse: Use to build the felt sense of massive, steady cashflow before the numbers reflect it.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from the Day 39 theme. The image is a river: wide, moving, never empty.
Sit or lie down. Close your eyes. One slow breath.

Picture your bank account as a river. Not a trickle: wide, steady, moving fast enough that you can hear it. Money moves through in constant inflow. Large deposits arrive without fanfare. The current does not stop between them.
Watch the balance number. It climbs through five figures, through six. It does not spike and drop. It rises in a steady climb and stays there.
Notice what your body does when it watches this. Notice whether it relaxes or braces. Let it relax. The river does not run dry. The next inflow is already on its way.
Hold that image for two minutes. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.
